input.name: James
post: Shipped the since-last-post window for the daybook.

Wins — James shipped the since-last-post window on router-daybook. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed calendar day, anchored to the post's feed timestamp so it is correct on any machine he runs it on. The fallback to a seven-day window is in and labelled honestly.

Struggles — Clock skew and a missing checkpoint both had to clamp to the seven-day floor, and Codex's per-day session directories meant the collector walks each day in the range rather than doing a single bounds check. It took a few passes to get the edge cases right without an empty digest.
Insight — The Router's own record of your posts is a better anchor than a local file that starts empty, because it is already the source of truth and survives a fresh install.

Offering — @kai got "MLX Whisper running locally"¹ this week, and James also uses MLX Whisper for the voice-note feature in router-daybook, so the two are clearly working on the same thing. He can share his setup notes for the transcription sidecar.

Asking — James needs a second pair of eyes on the seven-day clamp logic, specifically whether a future-dated checkpoint should fall back to the floor or to now. He is looking for anyone who has shipped a since-last-event window and handled clock skew cleanly.

footnotes.excerpt: Got MLX Whisper running locally for voice notes today, the resident model is fast.

feedEntries.content: Got MLX Whisper running locally for voice notes today, the resident model is fast after warmup.
Building an in-app timeline and I keep mixing up my own posts with everyone else's.

input.workLog: router-daybook: since-last-post window, 7-day clamp, codex per-day walk. Uses MLX Whisper sidecar for voice answers.
